summary: Replaced the project-local GEKKO preprocessor define with MWCC's built-in __PPCGEKKO__ target define across build flags, runtime platform checks, Dolphin debug code, MSL header comments, and m2ctx preprocessing flags. Review pushed the change away from adding a compatibility abstraction in platform.h and toward direct find/replace use of __PPCGEKKO__, avoiding a redundant manual GEKKO define.
what_changed: Removed -DGEKKO from the main Makefile CFLAGS because MWCC already supplies the target-specific __PPCGEKKO__ define for the Gekko processor.
Removed -DGEKKO from tools/m2ctx/m2ctx.py preprocessing arguments so context generation follows the same target define assumptions as the build.
Changed Runtime/platform.h checks from defined(__MWERKS__) && defined(GEKKO) to defined(__MWERKS__) && defined(__PPCGEKKO__) when defining MWERKS_GEKKO.
Changed Runtime/platform.h Gekko register block guard from #ifdef GEKKO to #ifdef __PPCGEKKO__.
Changed src/dolphin/db/db.c GNUC/Gekko conditional from defined(__GNUC__) && defined(GEKKO) to defined(__GNUC__) && defined(__PPCGEKKO__).
Updated MSL stdbool.h and stddef.h todo comments from 'Assumes GEKKO' to 'Assumes __PPCGEKKO__'.
Changed a comment in src/dolphin/base/PPCArch.h from 'GEKKO registers' to 'Gekko registers', aligning the prose with the processor name rather than the old macro spelling.
smart_moves: Used the compiler-provided target macro instead of maintaining a duplicate project macro, reducing build configuration drift between normal compilation and preprocessing tools.
Kept m2ctx in sync with Makefile flags by removing the same -DGEKKO argument from both places.
Applied the rename broadly enough to cover runtime platform logic, Dolphin debug conditional compilation, and documentation comments rather than only build flags.
Accepted review feedback that a compatibility alias in platform.h would not remove the need for manual defines under non-MWCC compilers and would preserve unnecessary indirection.
decomp_lessons: Prefer real compiler/toolchain predefined macros when they exist; project-local aliases can obscure the actual matching environment and create extra manual setup points.
When changing a preprocessor convention, update both compile flags and context-generation tools such as m2ctx so decompilation analysis sees the same macro environment as the build.
Avoid adding compatibility abstraction layers unless they solve a real portability problem; otherwise a direct find/replace can make the codebase easier to search and reason about.
Small macro cleanups can affect matching-adjacent infrastructure because headers, platform guards, asm/debug code, and preprocessing scripts may all depend on the same condition.
naming_conventions: Use __PPCGEKKO__ for the Gekko target preprocessor condition instead of a custom GEKKO define.
Use MWERKS_GEKKO as the derived convenience macro only when both __MWERKS__ and __PPCGEKKO__ are defined.
Use 'Gekko' as human-readable prose for the processor name instead of all-caps GEKKO when not referring to a macro.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Removing -DGEKKO from Makefile and m2ctx relies on MWCC's -proc gekko target selection to provide __PPCGEKKO__ automatically.
For non-MWCC toolchains such as GCC paths in db.c, target-specific conditional code should key off __PPCGEKKO__ directly if that path needs to emulate or provide the same macro.
Keeping preprocessing flags consistent across build and m2ctx avoids mismatched context output caused by stale manually-defined macros.

review_feedback.comment: Suggested using direct find/replace instead of adding a platform.h alias from __PPCGEKKO__ to GEKKO.
Initially argued that GEKKO abstraction could help because GCC and other compilers would need the define manually.
Pointed out that users would need to manually define GEKKO either way, so they could manually define the compiler-style target macro instead.
Accepted the feedback and replaced all GEKKO uses directly.

follow_up_queries: Search for any remaining uses of GEKKO as a macro after PR 678.
Check whether non-MWCC build or analysis paths define __PPCGEKKO__ consistently when targeting Gekko.
Audit other project-local target macros that duplicate compiler predefined macros.
Verify m2ctx preprocessing flags stay synchronized with Makefile CFLAGS for future toolchain changes.
